name: elio
description: Snappy, batteries-included terminal file manager with rich previews, inline images, bulk actions, and trash support
readme_summary: Elio is a fast, feature-rich terminal file manager written in Rust. It offers a three-pane layout for efficient navigation, supports rich file previews including inline images in compatible terminals, and provides essential file management features like bulk actions, trash support, and customizable themes.
key_features: Rich file previews (text, code, documents, media)
Inline image rendering in supported terminals
primary_use_case: A terminal-based file manager for efficient file browsing, previewing, and management within the command-line interface.
cover_image_prompt: A conceptual illustration of a highly organized, futuristic library where books are represented as glowing data cubes. A skilled librarian, with a subtle digital interface projected around their hands, is effortlessly sorting, previewing, and moving these data cubes using intuitive gestures. Some cubes display vibrant images or code snippets as they are handled. The library's architecture subtly incorporates elements of a terminal interface, with glowing lines of code forming shelves. The overall mood is efficient, clean, and modern, with a focus on clarity and ease of use, embodying the 'elio' file manager's capabilities. 3D isometric illustration style.

name: roboto_origin
description: Roboto_origin Fully Open-Source DIY Humanoid Robot/萝博头原型机全开源手搓级人形机器人
readme_summary: ROBOTO_ORIGIN is a fully open-source DIY humanoid robot project that provides the complete R&D process, including structure, electronics, and training code. It aims to enable individuals to build and operate a running and jumping humanoid robot through DIY assembly and provided open-source resources.
key_features: Open-source training and deployment code for walking and running
primary_use_case: Enabling hobbyists and researchers to build, train, and deploy their own functional humanoid robots through open-source hardware designs, software, and documentation.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan in a bright, modern workshop, meticulously assembling a sleek, humanoid robot from a pile of intricate mechanical parts and glowing wires. The robot is shown mid-stride, with one leg forward, demonstrating its ability to walk. Subtle holographic blueprints and lines of code float around the artisan, indicating the open-source nature and software integration. The background features shelves filled with components and screens displaying ROS2 and Isaac Sim interfaces. The overall mood is one of creation and empowerment, with a clean, vibrant color palette. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clear detail and a sense of accomplishment.

name: mieru
description: mieru is a socks5 / HTTP / HTTPS proxy to bypass censorship. 見える是一款 socks5 / HTTP / HTTPS 网络代理翻墙工具。
readme_summary: Mieru is a Go-based proxy tool designed to bypass censorship by providing secure, encrypted tunnels for SOCKS5, HTTP, and HTTPS traffic. It utilizes strong encryption and anti-detection techniques to evade network filtering, making it difficult for censors to identify and block.
key_features: Provides SOCKS5, HTTP, and HTTPS proxy interfaces
Uses XChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption with username, password, and system time-based keys
Implements random padding and replay attack detection to evade censorship
Supports multiple users on a single server
Does not use TLS, eliminating the need for domain registration
primary_use_case: Bypassing internet censorship through encrypted network proxying.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, transparent tunnel made of glowing, interwoven data streams, allowing a small, determined boat labeled 'Mieru' to pass through a chaotic, stormy sea of red 'censorship' blocks. On the other side of the storm, the boat emerges into a calm, clear ocean representing unrestricted internet access. Subtle visual cues like encrypted data packets flowing within the tunnel and a small, unobtrusive UI element showing connection status on the boat would hint at the software's function. The overall mood should be one of overcoming obstacles and achieving freedom, rendered in a vibrant, digital painting style with clear outlines and a focus on the contrast between the storm and the calm.

name: Shadowbroker
description: Open-source intelligence for the global theater. Track everything from the corporate/private jets of the wealthy, and spy satellites, to seismic events in one unified interface. Hook an AI agent up to have it parse through data and find previously unseen correlations. The knowledge is available to all but rarely aggregated in the open, until now.
readme_summary: ShadowBroker is a decentralized, real-time geospatial intelligence platform that aggregates data from over 60 live intelligence feeds into a unified dark-ops map interface. It visualizes aircraft, ships, satellites, conflict zones, and more, with options for AI agent integration for advanced data correlation.
key_features: Aircraft, ships, satellites, conflict zones, CCTV, GPS jamming, mesh radio, geopolitical events tracking
Server-side recon toolkit (DNS, WHOIS, BGP, IP sweep)
Multiple visual modes (DEFAULT, SATELLITE, FLIR, NVG, CRT)
Country dossiers, head-of-state lookup, entity-graph expansion
No accounts, product telemetry, or analytics
Self-hosted backend with SSRF guards and local-operator auth
OpenClaw / agent command channel for AI integration
primary_use_case: Aggregating and visualizing diverse, real-time public global intelligence data in a single interface for analysts, researchers, and radio operators, with capabilities for AI-assisted correlation.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant cartographer in a dimly lit, high-tech command center, meticulously drawing on a massive, holographic globe. The globe displays a dynamic, real-time overlay of global data streams: tiny airplane icons with flight paths, ship silhouettes on ocean currents, satellite orbits, and pulsing red zones indicating conflict. The cartographer uses a glowing stylus, transforming scattered data points into a cohesive, interconnected map. Subtle digital particles representing OSINT data flow from various sources towards the globe. Small, transparent screens around the room show snippets of code and data feeds. The repository name 'ShadowBroker' is subtly etched onto the cartographer's console. The scene is rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a dark, atmospheric color scheme and dramatic, focused lighting, emphasizing the unification of disparate global information.
